issues.title: Brand-linked FDA recall
issues.short: FDA investigated recalled WanaBana, Schnucks, and Weis apple cinnamon pouches linked to elevated lead and chromium.
issues.details: This is a brand/product-line red flag: FDA investigated recalled apple cinnamon puree/applesauce pouches from WanaBana, Schnucks, and Weis after reports of elevated blood lead levels and product testing that found elevated lead and chromium.

evidence.summary: FDA investigation and recall of WanaBana, Schnucks, and Weis apple cinnamon puree/applesauce pouches for elevated lead and chromium.

sources.name: FDA investigation: elevated lead and chromium in cinnamon applesauce pouches

issues.title: Elevated lead in ground cinnamon
issues.short: FDA found elevated lead in multiple ground cinnamon products and advised consumers to stop using listed products.
issues.details: FDA testing found elevated lead in listed ground cinnamon products sold under several brands. FDA said prolonged exposure may be unsafe and advised consumers to discard affected products.

evidence.summary: FDA public health alert for listed ground cinnamon products with elevated lead levels.

sources.name: FDA alert: certain cinnamon products due to elevated lead

issues.title: FDA illness investigation and recall
issues.short: FDA warned consumers not to eat any Diamond Shruumz products after illnesses, hospitalizations, and a recall.
issues.details: FDA and partners investigated illnesses associated with Diamond Shruumz chocolate bars, cones, and gummies. FDA said all flavors were recalled and consumers should not eat, sell, or serve them.

evidence.summary: FDA investigation of illnesses linked to Diamond Shruumz products; all flavors recalled.

sources.name: FDA investigation: Diamond Shruumz-brand chocolate bars, cones, and gummies

issues.title: E. coli outbreak linked to onions
issues.short: FDA linked an E. coli O157:H7 outbreak to recalled yellow onions served on McDonald's Quarter Pounders in affected states.
issues.details: FDA, CDC, and state partners investigated a multistate E. coli outbreak. FDA says traceback evidence showed recalled Taylor Farms yellow onions served on McDonald's Quarter Pounders were the likely source.

evidence.summary: FDA reported 104 illnesses, 34 hospitalizations, and 1 death in the McDonald's Quarter Pounder onion outbreak.

sources.name: FDA outbreak investigation: E. coli O157:H7 onions

issues.title: E. coli outbreak linked to organic carrots
issues.short: FDA linked recalled organic whole and baby carrots supplied by Grimmway Farms to an E. coli outbreak.
issues.details: FDA reported that recalled organic whole and baby carrots supplied by Grimmway Farms were the likely source of a multistate E. coli O121:H19 outbreak.

evidence.summary: FDA reported 48 illnesses, 20 hospitalizations, and 1 death in the organic carrots outbreak.

issues.title: Inorganic arsenic above action level
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists multiple apple juice brands recalled for inorganic arsenic above the action level.
issues.details: Refresco Beverages recalled multiple apple juice products, including Great Value and other store brands, because inorganic arsenic was above the FDA action level set in guidance.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement report F-1795-2024 for apple juice products with inorganic arsenic above action level.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: apple juice inorganic arsenic

issues.title: Potential Salmonella in Quaker products
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists Quaker Chewy bars and related cereal/granola products recalled for potential Salmonella.
issues.details: The Quaker Oats Company recall covered multiple bars, granola, and cereal products due to potential Salmonella contamination.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement report F-0950-2024 for Quaker products recalled for potential Salmonella.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: Quaker potential Salmonella

issues.title: Undeclared milk allergen
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists Lay's potato chips recalled because milk was not declared on the label.
issues.details: Frito-Lay recalled certain Lay's potato chips for undeclared milk allergen. This is a serious issue for people with milk allergy.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement report F-0428-2025 for Lay's Classic Potato Chips with undeclared milk allergen.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: Lay's undeclared milk

issues.title: Listeria contamination in cheese products
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists Rizo Lopez / Don Francisco cheese products recalled for Listeria monocytogenes contamination.
issues.details: Rizo Lopez Foods recalled multiple cheese products and related brands for Listeria monocytogenes contamination.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement event 93814 includes Class I recalls for Rizo Lopez / Don Francisco cheese products due to Listeria.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: Rizo Lopez Listeria recalls

issues.title: Potential Salmonella in caramel popcorn
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists Palmer Candy and Meijer caramel popcorn products recalled for potential Salmonella.
issues.details: Palmer Candy recalled drizzled caramel corn and Meijer caramel popcorn snack bowls because of potential Salmonella contamination.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement report F-1671-2024 for Palmer Candy and Meijer popcorn products with potential Salmonella.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: Palmer Candy potential Salmonella

issues.title: Listeria found on harvest equipment
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists Dole / Marketside Classic Salad recalled after harvest equipment tested positive for Listeria.
issues.details: Dole Fresh Vegetables recalled Marketside Classic Salad after harvest equipment used for raw iceberg lettuce tested positive for Listeria monocytogenes.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement report F-0757-2022 for Marketside Classic Salad linked to Listeria found on harvest equipment.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: Dole / Marketside salad Listeria

issues.title: Potential Listeria in Boar's Head products
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists Boar's Head ham/sandwich products recalled for potential Listeria monocytogenes.
issues.details: FDA enforcement data includes a Boar's Head Grab and Go ham sandwich recall for potential Listeria monocytogenes.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement report F-1703-2024 for Boar's Head ham sandwich products with potential Listeria.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: Boar's Head potential Listeria

issues.title: Potential Salmonella in cucumbers
issues.short: FDA enforcement data lists Lipman/Dairyland cucumber products recalled for potential Salmonella contamination.
issues.details: Lipman Family Farms recalled cucumber products distributed under Lipman/Dairyland descriptions because of potential Salmonella contamination.

evidence.summary: FDA enforcement report H-0030-2025 for cucumber products with potential Salmonella contamination.

sources.name: FDA enforcement report: Lipman cucumber potential Salmonella
